embedded-cpp/bare-metal-c.title: Bare Metal C
embedded-cpp/bare-metal-c.description: Bare metal means no operating system. Your code is the first thing that runs after the processor comes out of reset. There is no kernel to set up the stack, no libc to initialize the heap, no scheduler to give your tasks CPU time. You are in direct control of — and directly responsible for — every cycle.

embedded-cpp/deterministic-cpp.title: Deterministic C++
embedded-cpp/deterministic-cpp.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Embedded C/C++ section.

embedded-cpp/embedded-toolchains.title: Embedded Toolchains
embedded-cpp/embedded-toolchains.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Embedded C/C++ section.

embedded-cpp/fixed-point-math.title: Fixed-Point Math
embedded-cpp/fixed-point-math.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Embedded C/C++ section.

embedded-cpp/interrupt-driven-design.title: Interrupt-Driven Design
embedded-cpp/interrupt-driven-design.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Embedded C/C++ section.

embedded-cpp/memory-management.title: Memory Management
embedded-cpp/memory-management.description: In flight software, the heap is banned. Static allocation only — memory usage is determined at link time, and it never changes.

embedded-cpp/misra-c.title: MISRA-C
embedded-cpp/misra-c.description: MISRA-C (Motor Industry Software Reliability Association C) is a set of C language coding guidelines originally written for automotive systems. It has become the dominant safety-critical C standard across aerospace, medical devices, railway, and defense.

embedded-cpp/overview.title: Embedded C/C++ Overview
embedded-cpp/overview.description: The first thing to understand about embedded C/C++ for space systems: it is not a restricted subset of "real" C++. It is a different discipline with different constraints, different idioms, and different failure modes.

embedded-cpp/testing-embedded.title: Testing Embedded
embedded-cpp/testing-embedded.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Embedded C/C++ section.

embedded-cpp/volatile-and-registers.title: Volatile & Registers
embedded-cpp/volatile-and-registers.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Embedded C/C++ section.

flight-software/boot-sequence.title: Boot Sequence
flight-software/boot-sequence.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Flight Software section.

flight-software/cfs-framework.title: NASA Core Flight System (cFS)
flight-software/cfs-framework.description: cFS is NASA's open-source, reusable flight software framework. It defines an architecture, not just a library — a complete way of structuring spacecraft software that has flown on dozens of missions.

flight-software/command-telemetry.title: Command & Telemetry
flight-software/command-telemetry.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Flight Software section.

flight-software/data-stores.title: Data Stores
flight-software/data-stores.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Flight Software section.

flight-software/fault-management.title: Fault Management (FDIR)
flight-software/fault-management.description: Fault Detection, Isolation, and Recovery (FDIR) is the system that keeps a spacecraft alive when things go wrong — and things always go wrong.

flight-software/ground-interface.title: Ground Interface
flight-software/ground-interface.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Flight Software section.

flight-software/health-safety.title: Health & Safety
flight-software/health-safety.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Flight Software section.

flight-software/overview.title: Flight Software Architecture Overview
flight-software/overview.description: Flight software (FSW) is the software running on the spacecraft itself — the on-board computer (OBC) — that controls the vehicle, processes sensor data, executes mission objectives, responds to ground commands, and manages faults autonomously.

flight-software/software-updates.title: Software Updates
flight-software/software-updates.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Flight Software section.

flight-software/state-machines.title: State Machines
flight-software/state-machines.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Flight Software section.

intro.title: Space Embedded Systems
intro.description: This reference covers the disciplines required to build, understand, and contribute to flight software and space systems — from the C code running on a radiation-hardened microcontroller to the orbital mechanics equations that determine whether your spacecraft is pointing the right direction.

orbital-physics/adcs-software.title: ADCS Software
orbital-physics/adcs-software.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Orbital Physics section.

orbital-physics/attitude-representation.title: Attitude Representation
orbital-physics/attitude-representation.description: Attitude is the orientation of a spacecraft's body frame relative to a reference frame. Representing, tracking, and controlling attitude is the central problem of the ADCS system.

orbital-physics/coordinate-frames.title: Coordinate Frames
orbital-physics/coordinate-frames.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Orbital Physics section.

orbital-physics/kalman-filter.title: Kalman Filter
orbital-physics/kalman-filter.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Orbital Physics section.

orbital-physics/keplerian-orbits.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Orbital Physics section.

orbital-physics/link-budget.title: Link Budget
orbital-physics/link-budget.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Orbital Physics section.

orbital-physics/orbit-propagation.title: Orbit Propagation
orbital-physics/orbit-propagation.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Orbital Physics section.

orbital-physics/overview.title: Orbital Physics Overview
orbital-physics/overview.description: Flight software engineers implement physics. The attitude determination algorithm you write encodes rotational kinematics. The orbit propagator you integrate encodes gravitational dynamics. The time conversion function you call encodes relativistic corrections accumulated over decades of engineering by the GPS system.

orbital-physics/relativistic-effects.title: Relativistic Effects
orbital-physics/relativistic-effects.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Orbital Physics section.

orbital-physics/time-systems.title: Time Systems
orbital-physics/time-systems.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Orbital Physics section.

reference/companies.title: Space Companies
reference/companies.description: The landscape of companies building spacecraft software infrastructure — what they build, what problems they solve, and what kind of engineering they hire for.

reference/external-resources.title: External Resources
reference/external-resources.description: The authoritative references behind this documentation.

reference/glossary.title: Aerospace Glossary
reference/glossary.description: Aerospace has more acronyms per sentence than any other field. This is the reference.

reference/latency-numbers.title: Latency & Timing Numbers
reference/latency-numbers.description: The numbers every space systems engineer should have internalized. Not for memorization — for intuition about what your code is doing and what is physically possible.

reference/standards-index.title: Standards Index
reference/standards-index.description: The standards ecosystem for space software — what each one covers and when it applies.

rtos/freertos.title: FreeRTOS
rtos/freertos.description: FreeRTOS is the most widely used RTOS in the embedded world. For spacecraft, it is the dominant choice for CubeSats, small satellite OBCs, and subsystem controllers that don't require the full heritage of VxWorks or RTEMS.

rtos/ipc-patterns.title: IPC Patterns
rtos/ipc-patterns.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the RTOS section.

rtos/overview.title: Real-Time Operating Systems Overview
rtos/overview.description: A Real-Time Operating System (RTOS) is not just a small Linux. It is a fundamentally different kind of software — one that makes hard timing guarantees about when tasks execute, how long they take, and what happens when two tasks compete for a resource.

rtos/posix-psp.title: POSIX PSP
rtos/posix-psp.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the RTOS section.

rtos/rtems.title: RTEMS
rtos/rtems.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the RTOS section.

rtos/scheduling.title: Real-Time Scheduling
rtos/scheduling.description: The scheduler is the heart of an RTOS. It decides which task runs, when, and for how long. Understanding scheduling theory is essential for designing a spacecraft flight software architecture that meets its timing requirements.

rtos/timing-analysis.title: Timing Analysis
rtos/timing-analysis.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the RTOS section.

rtos/vxworks.title: VxWorks
rtos/vxworks.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the RTOS section.

rtos/watchdog-patterns.title: Watchdog Patterns
rtos/watchdog-patterns.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the RTOS section.

rtos/zephyr.title: Zephyr
rtos/zephyr.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the RTOS section.

safety-reliability/formal-verification.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Safety & Reliability section.

safety-reliability/overview.title: Safety & Reliability Overview
safety-reliability/overview.description: Writing the code is the easy part. Proving the code is correct — that it does what it is supposed to do, under all conditions, including ones you didn't anticipate — is the hard part.

safety-reliability/redundancy-patterns.title: Redundancy Patterns
safety-reliability/redundancy-patterns.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Safety & Reliability section.

space-hardware/cubesat-hardware.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Space Hardware section.

space-hardware/fpga-in-space.title: FPGAs in Space
space-hardware/fpga-in-space.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Space Hardware section.

space-hardware/microcontrollers.title: Microcontrollers
space-hardware/microcontrollers.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Space Hardware section.

space-hardware/overview.title: Space Hardware Overview
space-hardware/overview.description: Your code runs on hardware. In space, that hardware operates in one of the harshest environments accessible to humans: hard vacuum, extreme thermal cycling, and a constant rain of high-energy particles that can flip bits in your memory and latch up your transistors.

space-hardware/power-systems.title: Power Systems
space-hardware/power-systems.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Space Hardware section.

space-hardware/radiation-effects.title: Radiation Effects on Electronics
space-hardware/radiation-effects.description: The space radiation environment is the most consequential physical constraint for spacecraft software engineers. Every architectural decision about fault tolerance, memory scrubbing, redundancy, and safe mode exists in response to radiation.

space-hardware/radiation-hardening.title: Radiation Hardening
space-hardware/radiation-hardening.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Space Hardware section.

space-hardware/sensors-actuators.title: Sensors & Actuators
space-hardware/sensors-actuators.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Space Hardware section.

space-hardware/spacecraft-buses.title: Spacecraft Buses
space-hardware/spacecraft-buses.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Space Hardware section.

space-hardware/thermal-vacuum.title: Thermal Vacuum
space-hardware/thermal-vacuum.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Space Hardware section.

synthesis/attitude-control-loop.title: Attitude Control Loop
synthesis/attitude-control-loop.description: The attitude determination and control system (ADCS) is the most timing-critical and algorithmically complex subsystem in most spacecraft. It runs continuously, at a fixed rate, and any fault in its execution can cause the spacecraft to tumble.

synthesis/ccsds-parser.title: CCSDS Parser
synthesis/ccsds-parser.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Synthesis section.

synthesis/ecc-memory-scrubber.title: ECC Memory Scrubber
synthesis/ecc-memory-scrubber.description: A memory scrubber is a background task that periodically reads every byte of protected SRAM, allowing the ECC hardware to detect and correct single-bit errors before a second bit flip in the same word makes the error uncorrectable.

synthesis/orbit-propagator.title: Orbit Propagator
synthesis/orbit-propagator.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Synthesis section.

synthesis/overview.title: Synthesis Overview
synthesis/overview.description: This section takes real spacecraft software components and implements them fully — showing exactly how embedded C/C++, RTOS primitives, hardware constraints, orbital physics, and safety requirements interact in a single implementation.

synthesis/power-budget-manager.title: Power Budget Manager
synthesis/power-budget-manager.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Synthesis section.

synthesis/safe-mode-fsm.title: Safe Mode FSM
synthesis/safe-mode-fsm.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Synthesis section.

synthesis/telemetry-pipeline.title: Telemetry Pipeline
synthesis/telemetry-pipeline.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Synthesis section.

synthesis/tmr-voter.title: Triple Modular Redundancy Voter
synthesis/tmr-voter.description: Triple Modular Redundancy (TMR) is the oldest and most reliable fault-tolerance technique in safety-critical systems. Run the same computation three times independently, compare the results, and output the majority.

synthesis/watchdog-supervisor.title: Watchdog Supervisor
synthesis/watchdog-supervisor.description: Full content coming soon. This page is part of the Synthesis section.
